feed.title: Winden Tokens
feed.subtitle: Professional Design Token Management for Figma

entry.title: Version 1.1.0 Released
entry.content:
Release version 1.1.0 is now available!

💄 UI/Style Changes

  • dark ui (21360a5)
  • updated UI (739bd0e)
  • Rebuild styles: update relationships (68f1d57)

🔨 Code Refactoring

  • clean up (e9e8c3b)
  • clean up (e88c4b1)

🔄 Other Changes

  • Update manifest.json (44a16b7)
  • dark mode (a6ab65b)
  • card options. (e886b4c)
  • history (c924b5c)
  • sidebar (485e408)

Installation

To update to this version in Figma:

  1. Go to Plugins > Development > Import plugin from manifest
  2. Select the updated manifest.json file
  3. Or pull the latest code and run npm run build

Getting Started

If you’re new to Winden Tokens, check out our documentation and tutorials.

Have feedback or questions? Open an issue on GitHub
entry.summary: Dark mode UI update with improved relationships view. This release includes updated UI styling, dark theme support, cleaner code organization, and enhanced visual relationships between design tokens.

entry.title: Building Themes with Winden Tokens: Light Mode, Dark Mode & Beyond

link.title: Building Themes with Winden Tokens: Light Mode, Dark Mode & Beyond

entry.content:
Overview

Learn how to create and manage themes using Winden Tokens. This tutorial covers multi-mode variables, theme switching, and building flexible design systems that support light mode, dark mode, and custom themes.

What You’ll Learn

In this tutorial, you’ll discover:

  • Theme Creation: Building theme systems with Winden Tokens
  • Multi-Mode Variables: Working with Figma’s variable modes for theming
  • Theme Management: Organizing and maintaining multiple themes
  • Token Architecture: Structuring tokens for theme flexibility
  • Best Practices: Professional approaches to theme implementation

Key Theming Topics

This video explores essential theming concepts:

  • Setting up theme collections and modes
  • Creating semantic token layers
  • Implementing light and dark themes
  • Building custom brand themes
  • Managing theme-specific token values
  • Testing and validating themes across components

Why Theming Matters

Theming is crucial for modern design systems:

  • User Preferences: Support light/dark mode preferences
  • Brand Flexibility: Easy brand customization and white-labeling
  • Accessibility: Create high-contrast and accessible themes
  • Consistency: Maintain design consistency across themes
  • Efficiency: Change themes globally without redesigning

Theme Architecture Best Practices

Structure your tokens for maximum flexibility:

  1. Primitive Tokens: Base values (colors, sizes)
  2. Semantic Tokens: Purpose-based tokens (background, text, border)
  3. Component Tokens: Component-specific overrides
  4. Theme Modes: Light, dark, and custom themes

Getting Started with Theming

Prerequisites for this tutorial:

  • Winden Tokens installed in Figma
  • Understanding of basic variable concepts
  • Familiarity with design token patterns

If you need to install Winden Tokens:

  1. Go to Plugins > Development > Import plugin from manifest in Figma
  2. Select the manifest.json file from the GitHub repository
  3. Or build from source using npm run build

Common Theming Patterns

Light/Dark Theme Pattern

Primitive Collection:
- gray-50, gray-100, ... gray-900
- brand-primary, brand-secondary

Semantic Collection (Light/Dark modes):
- background: gray-50 (light) / gray-900 (dark)
- text: gray-900 (light) / gray-50 (dark)
- primary: brand-primary (both)

Multi-Brand Pattern

Brand Collection (Brand A/Brand B modes):
- primary: blue-600 (A) / red-600 (B)
- secondary: blue-400 (A) / red-400 (B)

Implementation Steps

Follow this workflow for theme implementation:

  1. Plan Your Theme Strategy: Define required themes and token structure
  2. Create Primitive Tokens: Establish base color and size values
  3. Build Semantic Layer: Create purpose-based tokens with modes
  4. Apply to Components: Use semantic tokens in your components
  5. Test Themes: Validate all themes across your design system
  6. Document: Create theme documentation for your team

Related Resources

  • Design Tokens Best Practices
  • Advanced Features Tutorial
  • Winden Tokens Introduction
  • Complete Documentation
  • Latest Release Notes

Theming Tips & Tricks

  • Use Aliases: Reference primitive tokens in semantic tokens
  • Test Early: Check themes as you build components
  • Name Consistently: Use clear, purpose-based names
  • Document Modes: Clearly label each theme mode
  • Version Control: Track theme changes carefully

Real-World Applications

Theming is valuable for:

  • Multi-brand product families
  • White-label applications
  • Accessibility compliance (high-contrast themes)
  • User preference support (light/dark mode)
  • Seasonal or event-based themes

Continue Learning

Explore more theming topics:

  • Plugin Update
  • Advanced Features
  • All Tutorials

Have Questions?

If you have questions about theming:

  • Open an issue on GitHub
  • Watch more tutorials in our tutorial series
  • Check out our comprehensive documentation

Master theming and create flexible, scalable design systems with Winden Tokens!

entry.title: Version 1.0.0 Released
entry.content:
Release version 1.0.0 is now available!

✨ New Features

  • Add managed step generator for numbers (2ea0b18)
  • Add preset import, delete-all, and multi-mode support (ec7eddc)
  • Reuse existing variables and add type filters (35edc89)
  • Add CollectionFilters and update toolbar (a8e8c84)
  • Add managed shade generator & syncing (9524dd3)

🐛 Bug Fixes

  • Fix Arrange Grid positions being overwritten by polling (188b745)
  • Merge pull request #8 from krstivoja/fix/standalone-group-columns (7a344c4)
  • Fix Arrange Grid placing standalone groups in managed chain columns (5bd98db)
  • Fix standalone groups overlapping managed chain columns (08d51f6)
  • Fix collection filter to hide/show nodes instead of removing them (9e2fa8c)
  • fixed grid (023c720)
  • fixed contrast ux (da824a1)
  • Fixed updating instead of deleting and recreating shades. (7151bfc)

🔄 Other Changes

  • Compress standalone group depth columns to consecutive indices (be75bfa)
  • Revert column offset in Arrange Grid for standalone groups (2bca298)
  • Update .gitignore (03c6846)
  • Merge pull request #7 from krstivoja/claude/modest-thompson (4ab4476)
  • Migrate node graph from Rete.js/custom SVG to @xyflow/react (e135509)
  • numbers (38064fe)
  • shades (0f2c701)
  • Auto-create default variable collection (e492458)
  • Sort shade groups numerically and preserve order (3e9eea1)
  • Delete figma-deploy.yml (3bcfec9)

Installation

To update to this version in Figma:

  1. Go to Plugins > Development > Import plugin from manifest
  2. Select the updated manifest.json file
  3. Or pull the latest code and run npm run build

Getting Started

If you’re new to Winden Tokens, check out our documentation and tutorials.

Have feedback or questions? Open an issue on GitHub
entry.summary: Major milestone with managed shade generator, step generator for numbers, multi-mode support, and preset imports. Includes collection filters, type filters, and ability to reuse existing variables for powerful design token workflows.

entry.title: Advanced Winden Tokens Techniques: Power User Guide

link.title: Advanced Winden Tokens Techniques: Power User Guide

entry.content:
Overview

Dive deeper into Winden Tokens with this advanced tutorial. Explore powerful features and advanced techniques that will take your design token management to the next level.

What You’ll Learn

In this tutorial, you’ll discover:

  • Advanced Features: Powerful capabilities for experienced users
  • Complex Workflows: Handling sophisticated design system scenarios
  • Optimization Techniques: Making the most of Winden Tokens’ capabilities
  • Advanced Organization: Structuring large-scale design systems
  • Power User Tips: Expert-level shortcuts and techniques

Advanced Topics Covered

This video explores advanced aspects including:

  • Complex variable relationships and dependencies
  • Advanced collection management strategies
  • Scaling design tokens for large projects
  • Integration with development workflows
  • Advanced export and import techniques

Who This Tutorial Is For

This tutorial is ideal for:

  • Experienced Winden Tokens users
  • Design system architects
  • Teams managing complex design systems
  • Power users looking to optimize their workflow
  • Contributors and plugin developers

Prerequisites

Before watching this tutorial:

  • Have Winden Tokens installed and running
  • Familiarity with basic token management
  • Understanding of design system concepts
  • Experience with Figma variables

Level Up Your Skills

This tutorial builds on foundational knowledge:

  1. Watch the introduction tutorial first
  2. Practice basic workflows from the earlier tutorial
  3. Then dive into these advanced techniques

Getting Started

If you need to install Winden Tokens:

  1. Go to Plugins > Development > Import plugin from manifest in Figma
  2. Select the manifest.json file from the GitHub repository
  3. Or build from source using npm run build

Implementation Guide

After mastering these advanced features:

  1. Assess your current design system complexity
  2. Identify areas where advanced features can help
  3. Implement techniques gradually in your workflow
  4. Document your team’s advanced workflows
  5. Train team members on new capabilities

Related Resources

  • Basic Tutorial
  • Theming Tutorial
  • Installation Guide
  • Design Tokens Best Practices
  • Complete Documentation

Best Practices for Advanced Usage

When working with advanced features:

  • Plan Ahead: Structure your token architecture before scaling
  • Document Everything: Keep detailed records of complex relationships
  • Test Thoroughly: Validate changes across your design system
  • Version Control: Track changes to complex token structures
  • Team Communication: Ensure everyone understands advanced patterns

Continue Learning

Explore more advanced topics:

  • Theming and Multi-Mode Variables
  • Plugin Updates
  • All Tutorials

Have Questions?

If you have questions about advanced features:

  • Open an issue on GitHub
  • Watch more tutorials in our tutorial series
  • Check out our comprehensive documentation

Ready for more? Check out our theming tutorial to learn about multi-mode variables!

entry.title: Master Winden Tokens: Essential Workflows for Design Token Management

link.title: Master Winden Tokens: Essential Workflows for Design Token Management

entry.content:
Overview

This tutorial demonstrates essential workflows and features in Winden Tokens. Learn how to effectively manage design tokens and streamline your design system workflow in Figma.

What You’ll Learn

In this tutorial, you’ll discover:

  • Core Workflows: Essential processes for working with design tokens
  • Feature Deep Dive: In-depth look at key plugin capabilities
  • Practical Examples: Real-world scenarios and use cases
  • Productivity Tips: Shortcuts and techniques for faster token management
  • Best Practices: Recommended approaches for design system organization

Key Topics Covered

This video covers important aspects of Winden Tokens including:

  • Variable creation and management workflows
  • Collection organization strategies
  • Token naming conventions
  • Bulk operations and efficiency techniques
  • Integration with your existing design system

Who This Tutorial Is For

This tutorial is perfect for:

  • Designers working with design systems
  • Teams managing shared design tokens
  • Anyone looking to improve their Figma workflow
  • Design system maintainers and contributors

Getting Started

If you haven’t installed Winden Tokens yet:

  1. Go to Plugins > Development > Import plugin from manifest in Figma
  2. Select the manifest.json file from the GitHub repository
  3. Or build from source using npm run build

Apply What You’ve Learned

After watching this tutorial:

  1. Try implementing the workflows in your own projects
  2. Experiment with the features demonstrated
  3. Adapt the techniques to your team’s needs
  4. Share your learnings with your design team

Related Resources

  • Installation Guide
  • Winden Tokens Introduction
  • Design Tokens Best Practices
  • Complete Documentation
  • Latest Release Notes

Continue Learning

Check out more tutorials in the series:

  • Plugin Update
  • Theming Tutorial
  • All Tutorials

Have Questions?

If you have any questions about the topics covered in this tutorial:

  • Open an issue on GitHub
  • Watch more tutorials in our tutorial series
  • Check out our comprehensive documentation

Keep learning! More tutorials are available in our tutorial series.

entry.title: What’s New in Winden Tokens: Latest Features and Updates

link.title: What’s New in Winden Tokens: Latest Features and Updates

entry.content:
Overview

This tutorial provides a comprehensive walkthrough of the latest improvements and new features added to Winden Tokens. Whether you’re a new user or have been using the plugin for a while, this update video will help you understand what’s new and how to take advantage of the latest capabilities.

What You’ll Learn

In this tutorial, you’ll discover:

  • Latest Features: New capabilities added in recent releases
  • UI Improvements: Enhanced user interface and user experience updates
  • Workflow Enhancements: Improved workflows for design token management
  • Performance Updates: Optimizations that make the plugin faster and more efficient
  • Best Practices: Tips for using the new features effectively in your design system

Key Highlights

Some of the major updates covered in this video include:

  • Enhanced variable management capabilities
  • Improved collection organization
  • Better token visualization
  • New bulk editing features
  • Performance improvements for large design systems

Getting Started

If you haven’t installed Winden Tokens yet:

  1. Go to Plugins > Development > Import plugin from manifest in Figma
  2. Select the manifest.json file from the GitHub repository
  3. Or build from source using npm run build

Staying Up to Date

To keep up with the latest updates:

  • Check the Release Notes regularly
  • Subscribe to our YouTube channel for video tutorials
  • Follow the GitHub repository for code updates
  • Join the community discussions

Related Resources

  • Documentation
  • More Tutorials
  • Version 1.1.0 Release Notes
  • Version 1.0.0 Release Notes
  • Design Tokens Best Practices

Have Questions?

If you have any questions about the topics covered in this tutorial, feel free to:

  • Open an issue on GitHub
  • Watch more tutorials in our tutorial series
  • Check out our comprehensive documentation

Want to see more tutorials? Subscribe to our YouTube channel and enable notifications for the latest updates!

entry.title: Version 0.0.9 Released
entry.content:

entry.summary: Deployment update and stability improvements for the Winden Tokens Figma plugin.

entry.title: Version 0.0.8 Released
entry.content:
Release version 0.0.8 is now available!

🐛 Bug Fixes

  • fixed contast drop down fnt size and removed drag and drop as figma api does not support it (8c73dc7)
  • fixed drag and drop (fb3dae8)

🔄 Other Changes

  • Update release.yml (d91bcae)

Installation

To update to this version in Figma:

  1. Go to Plugins > Development > Import plugin from manifest
  2. Select the updated manifest.json file
  3. Or pull the latest code and run npm run build

Getting Started

If you’re new to Winden Tokens, check out our documentation and tutorials.

Have feedback or questions? Open an issue on GitHub

entry.content:

Installation

To update to this version in Figma:

  1. Go to Plugins > Development > Import plugin from manifest
  2. Select the updated manifest.json file
  3. Or pull the latest code and run npm run build
